steps.title: Interpret what the ratio 1:26 means
steps.content:
A ratio of coaches to athletes of 1:26 means: for every 1 coach, there are 26 athletes. In other words, the number of athletes is always 26 times the number of coaches:

$$\text{athletes} = 26 \times \text{coaches}$$

steps.title: Apply the relationship with x coaches
steps.content:
Since there are $x$ coaches at the track meet, substitute into the relationship:

$$\text{athletes} = 26x$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
